sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
After clicking on the red object, 6 blocks in the bottom row shifted. They all moved one position to the right. This looks like clicking on the red object causes a row shift. The bottom row (row 43-46) blocks shifted right by one position.
Wait, let me look more carefully:
- w: [43, 18] -> [43, 24] (shifted right by 6 cols = 1 block position)
- g: [43, 48] -> [43, 12] (wrapped around from right to left)
- S: [43, 36] -> [43, 42] (shifted right)
- b: [43, 30] -> [43, 36] (shift)
- p: [43, 42] -> [43, 48] (shift)
So the bottom row blocks shifted right by one position (rotated) right by one position. The g block wrapped from the rightmost to the leftmost position.
This is a puzzle where clicking on the red object causes the bottom row to rotate right.
Let me understand the grid better. The blocks are arranged in rows of 4x4 blocks. Let me figure out the full grid layout.
Looking at the boundaries, the blocks seem to be in a grid with positions at rows 19, 25, 31, 37, 43 and columns 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42, 48.
Let me think about the puzzle structure:
- There are colored blocks in a grid. The red object is on the left and green is on the right. Clicking on the red object causes the bottom row to rotate right.
The goal might be to align blocks of the same color or create a path from red to green.
Let me see what happens if I click on other objects. Let me try clicking on the green object to see if it causes a different row to shift.
